release-date.comment: Announced/unveiled May 31, 2023; shipping started shortly after.

status.comment: Product page still actively listed and stocked through resellers (Arace, Amazon) as of 2026-04-26.

architecture-family.comment: RV64GC ISA via SiFive U74 cores.

open-source-hardware.comment: Vendor publishes schematics (V1.21) and 2D dimension PDFs, but full PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license were not located. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Shenzhen MilkV Technology Co., Ltd, headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

cpu-cores.comment: Plus an S7 monitor core (not user-exposed); 4× U74-MC application cores.

process-node.comment: JH7110 is reported as fabricated on a 28 nm process; not stated explicitly on Milk-V's product page.

vulkan-support.comment: Vulkan 1.2 supported by the Imagination BXE-4-32 GPU; mainline open-source driver coverage remains limited (glmark2-es2 scored 0 in independent testing per geerlingguy/sbc-reviews #46).

hw-video-encode.comment: H.265 1080p@30 encode only. JPEG encoder also present but not in tag set.

ram-min-gb.comment: Vendor product page advertises 1/2/4/8 GB; retail availability typically starts at 2 GB.

emmc.comment: Plug-and-play eMMC module slot (module sold separately).

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 E-key slot is present (PCIe 2.0 x1) but intended for Wi-Fi/BT modules; NVMe storage is not supported on the Mars (unlike its Mars CM/M.2 M-key cousins).

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; M.2 E-key slot accepts a third-party Wi-Fi/BT module.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; provided only via optional M.2 E-key module.

poe-support.comment: PoE only via an add-on PoE expansion board.

max-display-resolution.comment: Vendor lists 4K@30 output (4K@60 decode); HDMI 2.0 port.

mipi-csi.comment: MIPI-CSI camera connector (2-lane); some sources also list a 4-lane CSI.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm analog audio jack present per WikiDevi teardown; not prominent on the marketing page.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin header; up to 28 usable GPIO at 3.3 V.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Form factor mirrors the Raspberry Pi 3B layout (85×56 mm) with compatible header. EEPROM-based HAT auto-config compliance not formally certified — mechanical compatibility yes, software-driver coverage for HAT-specific drivers depends on RISC-V kernel support.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Single PCIe 2.0 lane routed to the M.2 E-key slot.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, UART, PWM, and CAN exposed on the 40-pin header.

power-input.value: USB-C 5V/3A+ (also via GPIO header or PoE-HAT)

idle-power-w.comment: Measured at the wall by Jeff Geerling's sbc-reviews.

load-power-w.comment: Peak under stress-ng matrix and Geekbench 6 multicore (5.3 W); HPL averaged 4.7 W.

cooling-required.comment: Modest 28 nm SoC; runs warm under sustained load. Vendor does not formally mandate active cooling, but heatsinking is widely recommended in community reviews.

rtc-battery-header.comment: An RTC chip (HYM8563TS) is present; whether a battery-backed connector/holder is fitted is not clearly documented in vendor materials.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Adopts the same layout as the Raspberry Pi 3B per multiple reviews.

weight-g.comment: Vendor product page and reviews consulted (Tom's Hardware, XDA, tinycomputers.io, geerlingguy sbc-reviews) do not list bare-board weight.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: U-Boot upstream support since v2024.x and mainline Linux 6.10+ boots on Mars; GPU (BXE-4-32) is not yet upstream and PCIe support is incomplete (Wi-Fi cards/eGPUs unreliable). Hence 'partial' rather than 'full'.

official-os-image.comment: Milk-V publishes a Debian-based official image.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor ships a Debian Bookworm image. Ubuntu 24.04/25.04 RISC-V images are explicitly board-tested by Canonical. Fedora Server 42 (RISC-V) images boot on Mars. Armbian support is in-progress (community thread, not yet official). deepin V23 has also been adapted but is not in the tag set.

bootloader.comment: OpenSBI (M-mode firmware) chained into U-Boot (S-mode bootloader), standard JH7110 boot flow.

long-term-support.comment: Milk-V has not published a formal LTS window for the Mars; community kernel maintenance is improving via mainline (StarFive JH7110 platform), but vendor commitment is undefined.

android-support.comment: No official or maintained Android image listed for Mars; vendor focus is Linux/Debian-based.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP for the 2 GB configuration (the 1 GB tier is rarely retailed). Snapshot — pricing checked 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Milk-V's own store, Arace.tech (international), Amazon (NA), and various AliExpress sellers — effectively global.

typical-lead-time.comment: Stock available at multiple resellers as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate getting-started docs, schematics PDF, and dimension drawings; gaps in software-stack details (kernel matrix, distro caveats, peripheral driver state).

community-size.comment: Active but modest-size community (Milk-V Discord/forum, Reddit r/RISCV). Niche relative to mainstream ARM SBCs.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run community forum (community.milkv.io) with regular activity; Discord also exists.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: First-party PoE HAT, eMMC modules, heatsinks, and cases exist; broader RPi HAT compatibility is mechanical-only and depends on RISC-V driver availability — limits practical accessory depth.
